tools.name: restcountries_country_info
tools.description: [restcountries] Look up country information from REST Countries.

tools.name: fcdo_travel_advice
tools.description: [fcdo] Get UK FCDO travel advice for a specific country. Includes safety, entry requirements, health, and warnings.

tools.name: fcdo_list_countries
tools.description: [fcdo] List all countries with UK FCDO travel advice.

tools.name: airports_lookup
tools.description: Look up an airport by IATA (3-char) or ICAO (4-char) code. Returns full details including runways.

tools.name: airports_search
tools.description: Search airports by name or city. Returns up to 20 results sorted by size.

tools.name: airports_near
tools.description: Find airports near a geographic point. Returns results sorted by distance.

tools.name: airlines_lookup
tools.description: Look up an airline by IATA (2-char) or ICAO (3-char) code.

tools.name: airlines_search
tools.description: Search airlines by name. Returns up to 20 results.

tools.name: visa_check
tools.description: Check visa requirement for a passport country visiting a destination.

tools.name: visa_summary
tools.description: Overview of visa-free access for a passport country — counts by category.

tools.name: data_status
tools.description: Check which static datasets (airports, airlines, visas) are loaded and their row counts.

tools.name: peek_experience_availability
tools.description: [peek] Get availability information for a specific experience including dates, times, and pricing

tools.name: peek_experience_details
tools.description: [peek] Get detailed information about a specific experience by ID

tools.name: peek_list_tags
tools.description: [peek] List all category tags

tools.name: peek_render_activity_tiles
tools.description: [peek] Render activity tiles for a list of activity IDs, returning an embeddable widget URI

tools.name: peek_search_experiences
tools.description: [peek] Search for travel experiences with comprehensive filtering options. Returns available categories, tags, and regions with IDs for further filtering.

tools.name: peek_search_regions
tools.description: [peek] Search for regions by name

tools.name: tourradar_b2b-operator-details
tools.description:
[tourradar] Use this when the user wants information about a tour operator, such as their terms and policies.

Fetches operator details including name, code, and terms & conditions based on operator ID.

tools.name: tourradar_b2b-cities-search
tools.description:
[tourradar] Use this when you need to find city IDs for tour search filters like start city, end city, or cities to visit.

Searches for cities by name within a specific country. Supports multiple name variants to handle different spellings (e.g., Krakow, Kraków, Cracow).

tools.name: tourradar_b2b-continents-list
tools.description:
[tourradar] Use this when you need continent IDs for filtering tours by region.

Returns a list of all supported continents with their IDs and names for use in tour search filters.

tools.name: tourradar_b2b-countries-list
tools.description:
[tourradar] Use this when you need to look up country IDs for filtering tours or validating country names.

Returns a complete list of all supported countries with their IDs, names, and ISO country codes.

tools.name: tourradar_b2b-currencies-list
tools.description:
[tourradar] Use this when you need to check supported currencies or get currency details like symbols.

Returns a list of all supported currencies with their codes (USD, EUR), names, and symbols ($, €).

tools.name: tourradar_b2b-languages-list
tools.description:
[tourradar] Use this when you need language IDs for filtering tours by guide language.

Returns a list of all supported languages with their IDs, codes, and names for use in tour search filters.

tools.name: tourradar_b2b-tour-types-list
tools.description:
[tourradar] Use this when you need tour type IDs for filtering tours by category like adventure, cultural, or wildlife.

Returns a hierarchical list of tour type groups and their individual types with IDs and names.

tools.name: tourradar_b2b-tour-departures
tools.description:
[tourradar] Use this when the user asks about available departure dates, pricing for specific dates, or needs to validate if a departure date is available.

Returns a list of departures for a specific tour within a date range, including availability status, pricing, and booking information.

tools.name: tourradar_b2b-tour-details
tools.description:
[tourradar] Use this when the user wants to see detailed information about a specific tour.

Fetches comprehensive tour details including itinerary, pricing, operator info, images, and booking links based on tour ID.

tools.name: tourradar_b2b-tour-faq
tools.description:
[tourradar] Use this when the user has questions about a tour that might be answered in the FAQ section.

Returns a paginated list of frequently asked questions and answers about a specific tour, covering topics like inclusions, requirements, and policies.

tools.name: tourradar_b2b-tour-map
tools.description:
[tourradar] Use this when the user wants to see tour routes on a map or compare itineraries visually.

Fetches tour details for one or more tours and prepares map data with all itinerary locations, coordinates, and day-by-day information for visual display.

tools.name: tourradar_general-current-date
tools.description:
[tourradar] Use this when you need to know the current date, especially before setting departure date filters.

Returns the current date and time in ISO format. Essential for calculating valid date ranges for tour searches.

tools.name: tourradar_vertex-tour-search
tools.description:
[tourradar] Use this when the user describes what they want in natural language and you need AI-powered semantic search to understand their intent.

Before use this tool, please READ all possible filters.
PLESE USE FILTERS, when can be used, to make search faster and much more precise.
Please use st

tools.name: tourradar_vertex-tour-title-search
tools.description: [tourradar] Search for tours by title using AI-powered semantic search. Returns a list of matching tour IDs and titles. Use this when you need to look up a tour by name. When you know tour id, use b2b-tour-details tool to display details about specific tour

tools.name: tourradar_vertex-review-search
tools.description: [tourradar] Search tour reviews using AI-powered semantic search. Requires tourIds to scope results to specific tours. Use this when the user asks about reviews, feedback, or experiences for specific tours. Combine with an optional text query to find reviews mentioning specific topics (e.g., 'food',

tools.name: tourradar_web-ask-question
tools.description:
[tourradar] Use this when the user wants to ASK A QUESTION about a tour or operator WITHOUT committing to a booking.

Two modes:
- mode "tour": a question about a specific tour. Requires `tourId`. The `departureDate` is OPTIONAL here — if the customer has not picked a date, omit it and the backend w

tools.name: tourradar_web-tour-booking
tools.description:
[tourradar] Use this when the user explicitly wants to book a tour and has provided their contact details.

Creates a booking request for a specific tour. Requires real contact details (email, names; phone optional). Always confirm the departure date by checking departures first. Never fabricate or

tools.name: tourradar_web-tour-send-brochure
tools.description:
[tourradar] Use this when the user requests a tour brochure or PDF to be sent to their email.

Sends a tour brochure (PDF file) to the specified email address. Before using this, ensure the user has provided their email address. If not provided, ask the user for it. Never generate or assume email ad

tools.name: tourradar_algolia-operator-search
tools.description:
[tourradar] Use this when you need to find a tour operator's ID by their name for filtering tours.

Searches tour operators by name and returns matching operators with their IDs. Use the returned operator ID with tour search filters.

tools.name: trivago_trivago-accommodation-radius-search
trivago_trivago-accommodation-search
tools.description: [trivago] Search for accommodations and hotels by destination or point of interest on trivago. Additional Information: {"knownInformation":{"currentYear":"2026","today":"2026-07-15"}}

tools.name: skiplagged_sk_flights_search
tools.description: [skiplagged] Search Skiplagged for flights between specific locations with filtering options for passengers, fare class, stops, airlines, and timing preferences

tools.name: skiplagged_sk_destinations_anywhere
tools.description: [skiplagged] Find cheapest destinations from a departure city when flexible about where to go. Perfect for discovering travel opportunities and deals.

tools.name: skiplagged_sk_flex_departure_calendar
tools.description: [skiplagged] Generate a flexible calendar of the lowest one-way fares around a chosen departure, returning date → cheapest-price entries to help pick the best day to fly. Intended for flexible-date price discovery, not exact itinerary selection.

tools.name: skiplagged_sk_flex_return_calendar
tools.description: [skiplagged] Generate a flexible round-trip price calendar for a fixed-length stay around a chosen travel window. Returns (depart date, return date, lowest total price) entries for nearby date pairs that preserve the original trip length; intended for price discovery, not exact itinerary selection.

tools.name: skiplagged_sk_hotels_search
tools.description: [skiplagged] Search Skiplagged for hotels in a city with specific check-in and check-out dates, including ratings and pricing.

tools.name: skiplagged_sk_cars_search
tools.description: [skiplagged] Search Skiplagged for rental cars between pickup and dropoff locations/dates, returning prices, companies, and car details.

tools.name: skiplagged_sk_hotel_details
tools.description: [skiplagged] Fetch room-level availability, pricing, and amenities for a specific hotel and stay dates.

tools.name: skiplagged_sk_faq_search
tools.description: [skiplagged] Search FAQ for relevant answers about Skiplagged - company, stuff, its products, and policies. Useful for customer support and general inquiries

tools.name: skiplagged_sk_resolve_location
tools.description: [skiplagged] Resolve latitude and longitude to the IATA code of nearest airport and city information

tools.name: skiplagged_sk_resolve_iata
tools.description: [skiplagged] Resolve a city name to a valid IATA code

tools.name: ferryhopper_get_ports
tools.description: [ferryhopper] Get a list of global ports and their details

tools.name: ferryhopper_get_disruptions
tools.description: [ferryhopper] Get a list of disruptions for a given date and country

tools.name: ferryhopper_get_direct_connections_for_ports
tools.description: [ferryhopper] Get a list of all the direct connections between ports

tools.name: ferryhopper_search_trips
tools.description: [ferryhopper] Get a list available ferry trips between two ports on a specific date

tools.name: travel_agent
tools.description: Ask a travel question in natural language. Routes to the right tools automatically and returns a combined answer. Example: 'Cheapest flights from Zurich to Rome next week, and do I need a visa?'

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "tourradar_vertex-tour-search" is 5173 chars, far longer than this server's median (154). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: [tourradar] Use this when the user describes what they want in natural language and you need AI-powered semantic search to understand their intent. Before use t…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "tourradar_web-ask-question" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: [tourradar] Use this when the user wants to ASK A QUESTION about a tour or operator WITHOUT committing to a booking. Two modes: - mode "tour": a question about …

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "tourradar_web-tour-booking" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: [tourradar] Use this when the user explicitly wants to book a tour and has provided their contact details. Creates a booking request for a specific tour. Requir…

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "tourradar_web-tour-send-brochure" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: [tourradar] Use this when the user requests a tour brochure or PDF to be sent to their email. Sends a tour brochure (PDF file) to the specified email address. B…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: tourradar_vertex-tour-search, tourradar_vertex-review-search · Private data: tourradar_web-ask-question, tourradar_web-tour-booking, tourradar_web-tour-send-brochure · Public sink: tourradar_web-ask-question, tourradar_web-tour-booking, tourradar_web-tour-send-brochure · Destructive: tourradar_general-current-date.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: tourradar_vertex-tour-search, tourradar_vertex-review-search · Private data: tourradar_web-ask-question, tourradar_web-tour-booking, tourradar_web-tour-send-brochure · Public sink: tourradar_web-ask-question, tourradar_web-tour-booking, tourradar_web-tour-send-brochure · Destructive: tourradar_general-current-date

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
